traveling_salesman_problem:
Function find_travelling_salesman_path(G):
ans ← infinity
n ← |V|
for start in V do:
length ← 0
for each u in V do:
State[u] ← 0
v ← start
State[v] ← 1
for i ← 1 to n-1 do:
select u in V that State[u] = 0 and G[v][u] is minimum
length ← length + G[v][u]
State[u] ← 1
v ← u
ans ← min(ans, length + G[v][start])
return ans
shortest_path_length:
Function find_shortest_path(G, start, end):
    Initialize queue Q: Q ← [(start, [start], 0)]
    Create set of visited nodes: V' ← {}
    While Q is not empty:
        (node, path, distance) ← Q.dequeue()
        If node is the end node:
        result ← path
            return distance
        Mark node as visited: add node to V'
        Iterate over all neighbors of node
            If neighbor is not in V':
                Enqueue (neighbor, list(path) + [neighbor], distance + 1) to Q
clustering:
FUNCTION ClusteringCoefficient_Weighted(edge_list, node):
    adj ← empty map from node to map(node → weight)
    w_max ← 0

    FOR EACH edge IN edge_list:
        IF edge IS NOT a list OR SIZE(edge) < 3:
            RETURN NONE

        u ← edge[0]
        v ← edge[1]
        w ← edge[2]

        ADD v WITH weight w TO adj[u]
        ADD u WITH weight w TO adj[v]

        IF w > w_max:
            w_max ← w

    IF w_max ≤ 0:
        RETURN 0

    IF node NOT IN adj:
        RETURN 0

    neighbors ← LIST of keys in adj[node]
    k ← SIZE(neighbors)

    IF k < 2:
        RETURN 0

    s ← 0

    FOR i FROM 0 TO k − 1:
        a ← neighbors[i]
        FOR j FROM i + 1 TO k − 1:
            b ← neighbors[j]

            IF b NOT IN adj[a]:
                CONTINUE

            w_na ← adj[node][a] / w_max
            w_nb ← adj[node][b] / w_max
            w_ab ← adj[a][b] / w_max

            s ← s + (w_na × w_nb × w_ab)^(1/3)

    RETURN s / (k × (k − 1))
endpoint_aware_flow_score:
FUNCTION ENDPOINT_AWARE_FLOW_SCORE(edges, s, t):

    # Build residual graph for max flow
    # and undirected neighbor sets for clustering
    CREATE empty map RESIDUAL
    CREATE empty map UNDIRECTED_NEIGHBORS

    FUNCTION ENSURE_NODE(x):
        IF x not in RESIDUAL:
            RESIDUAL[x] ← empty map
        IF x not in UNDIRECTED_NEIGHBORS:
            UNDIRECTED_NEIGHBORS[x] ← empty set

    FOR each directed edge (u, v, capacity) in edges:
        IF capacity < 0:
            RAISE error
        ENSURE_NODE(u)
        ENSURE_NODE(v)

        IF u == v:
            CONTINUE

        # Directed capacity for flow
        RESIDUAL[u][v] ← RESIDUAL[u][v] + capacity
        IF u not in RESIDUAL[v]:
            RESIDUAL[v][u] ← 0

        # Undirected projection for clustering
        ADD v to UNDIRECTED_NEIGHBORS[u]
        ADD u to UNDIRECTED_NEIGHBORS[v]

    ENSURE_NODE(s)
    ENSURE_NODE(t)

    # Local clustering coefficient on undirected projection
    FUNCTION CLUSTERING_COEFFICIENT(v):
        NEIGHBORS ← UNDIRECTED_NEIGHBORS[v]
        k_v ← SIZE(NEIGHBORS)
        IF k_v < 2:
            RETURN 0

        E_v ← 0
        FOR each unordered pair (a, b) in NEIGHBORS:
            IF b ∈ UNDIRECTED_NEIGHBORS[a]:
                E_v ← E_v + 1

        RETURN (2 × E_v) / (k_v × (k_v − 1))

    C_s ← CLUSTERING_COEFFICIENT(s)
    C_t ← CLUSTERING_COEFFICIENT(t)

    # Edmonds–Karp maximum flow algorithm
    FUNCTION MAX_FLOW(source, sink):
        IF source == sink:
            RETURN 0

        FLOW ← 0

        WHILE true:
            CREATE empty map PARENT
            CREATE empty queue Q
            PARENT[source] ← null
            ENQUEUE source into Q

            WHILE Q not empty AND sink not in PARENT:
                x ← DEQUEUE Q
                FOR each y in RESIDUAL[x]:
                    IF RESIDUAL[x][y] > 0 AND y not in PARENT:
                        PARENT[y] ← x
                        ENQUEUE y into Q

            IF sink not in PARENT:
                BREAK

            BOTTLENECK ← infinity
            v ← sink
            WHILE v ≠ source:
                u ← PARENT[v]
                BOTTLENECK ← min(BOTTLENECK, RESIDUAL[u][v])
                v ← u

            v ← sink
            WHILE v ≠ source:
                u ← PARENT[v]
                RESIDUAL[u][v] ← RESIDUAL[u][v] − BOTTLENECK
                RESIDUAL[v][u] ← RESIDUAL[v][u] + BOTTLENECK
                v ← u

            FLOW ← FLOW + BOTTLENECK

        RETURN FLOW

    F ← MAX_FLOW(s, t)

    # Endpoint-aware flow score
    SCORE ← F × (1 + (C_s + C_t) / 2)

    RETURN SCORE
